content.problemStatement:
Soldiers are gathered in NNN groups, and every group has exactly MMM soldiers with ids
from 111 to MMM in each group. Soldier with id jjj in ithi^{th}ith group has strength AijA_{ij}Aij​.

A soldier is **strongest** in his group if there exists no soldier who has strength greater than him. Similarly, a soldier is **weakest** among all soldiers with the same id as his if there exists no soldier who has strength less than him.

The general is interested in finding if there is a soldier that is strongest in his group but
weakest among all soldiers with the same id as his. Can you help him?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains 222 integers NNN denoting the number of groups and MMM denoting the number of soldiers in each group.
Followed by NNN lines, where ithi^{th}ith line contains MMM space separated integers Ai0,Ai1,Ai2,..,AiMA_{i_0}, A_{i_1}, A_{i_2} ,.., A_{i_M}Ai0​​,Ai1​​,Ai2​​,..,AiM​​ where AijA_{ij}Aij​ is the strength of the soldier in ithi^{th}ith group with id as jjj.


Output Format

For each test case, output a single line containing either "Yes" if there is at least one soldier satisfying the above mentioned criteria or "No" if there is no such soldier.
You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example: the strings "yes", "YeS", "YES" will be treated as the same strings).
content.inputFormat: The input consists of one or more test cases. Each test case starts with two integers N and M – the number of groups and the number of soldiers in each group. Then follow N lines, each containing M integers. The j‑th integer on the i‑th line (1‑based) is the strength A[i][j] of the soldier with id j in group i. The file ends at EOF.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if there exists at least one soldier who is the strongest in his own group and at the same time the weakest among all soldiers having the same id, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Group 1: strengths [1,1]; both are maximal in the group and minimal for their ids (id1 min=1, id2 min=1). Hence at least one soldier satisfies the condition.
No cell is simultaneously the maximum of its row and the minimum of its column.
The only soldier is both strongest in his group and weakest among his id.

solution.approach: For every group i compute the maximum strength maxInGroup[i] and remember the column(s) where this maximum occurs. For every id j (i.e. column) compute the minimum strength minInColumn[j]. After these two passes, scan the matrix once more: if a cell (i,j) satisfies A[i][j] == maxInGroup[i] && A[i][j] == minInColumn[j] then the required soldier exists. If any such cell is found output "Yes", otherwise "No". The algorithm uses two linear passes over the N×M matrix, thus O(N·M) time and O(N+M) extra space.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N+M) for the arrays of group maxima and column minima
